recent_calls.summary: The call was initiated by the user but no interaction occurred as the user did not respond to the agent's request for a ZIP code. The call ended with the user hanging up without providing any information.

The user, Rishan, called to follow up on an air conditioner service that was delayed since winter. The agent scheduled a service appointment for Tuesday morning and confirmed contact preferences. The user expressed satisfaction with the interaction and agreed to receive information about maintenance programs.

The user called after hours requesting to speak to a live person. The agent informed the user that live assistance was unavailable and offered to relay the message for follow-up on the next business day. The call ended without the user providing further details.

The user called to schedule a follow-up visit after accepting a cover plan, specifically for a furnace referral follow-up. The agent attempted to clarify scheduling details, but the user did not provide a preferred date or time. The call ended with the user hanging up before finalizing the appointment.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly after the agent introduced herself and began to ask how she could help. The user did not respond or complete the conversation.

Regina from American Home Shield called to provide a dispatch number and request a follow-up on a technician's report for a heating system service. The agent confirmed the details, corrected the service address, and noted Regina's preference for a callback. Regina declined information about maintenance programs and gave positive feedback on the interaction.

The user scheduled a heating and cooling installation for a new air conditioner and furnace, preferring a Monday appointment. The agent confirmed the service address and callback number, noted no special access instructions, and collected user feedback on the AI assistant. The call concluded successfully with the user's positive feedback and no issues.

Jim called to cancel his appointment scheduled for tomorrow afternoon at 1635 Boonslick in Saint Charles. The agent confirmed the cancellation and collected feedback from Jim, who rated the experience a 4 out of 5 and mentioned the interaction was a little repetitive. The cancellation request was successfully processed for the next business day.

The user, Kara Karraker, called to schedule a heating service appointment due to their furnace turning off and on quickly and not completing a full cycle. The agent confirmed the details and noted the user's preference for the earliest available appointment. The user declined to provide feedback, and the call ended with confirmation of the service request.
The user called after hours asking if CG or Amy were still at the office. The agent informed the user that the office was closed and offered assistance, but the user decided to call back on Monday.
The call was initiated by the user but ended abruptly after a brief greeting from the agent without any further conversation.
The user returned a call from Budget and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent facilitated the transfer without any issues.
The user requested to speak with a live agent and the call was successfully transferred to another agent. The interaction was brief and ended with the transfer.

The user called Budget to speak with someone about a proposal they received and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call was brief and ended with a successful transfer to another agent.
The user returned a call and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent facilitated the transfer without any issues.
The agent greeted the user and attempted to identify the purpose of the call to transfer them to the appropriate person. The call was then successfully transferred to another number.

The user called back after being disconnected from a previous call and asked to be connected with the same person. The agent apologized for the disconnect and offered to pass along a message or assist with the user's needs.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user returned a call from Budget and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, facilitated the transfer without any issues.
The call was very brief and ended abruptly after the agent greeted the user and the user responded with a short acknowledgment. No further conversation or assistance took place.

The user, Joel, contacted Budget and was quickly transferred to a human agent upon request. The initial AI agent confirmed the transfer and ensured the user wanted to speak with a live agent before completing the transfer. The call ended with a successful transfer to a human agent.

The call was initiated by Beck from American Home Shield to follow up on a work order assigned to the company. The agent, Sophie, responded but the user did not continue the conversation, and the call ended shortly after.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for their heating and cooling system, which is off due to water damage. The agent offered available slots, and the user agreed to a provisional booking for Thursday, April 16th at 10 AM. The call ended with the user declining to provide feedback.

The user called to set up a service appointment for their air conditioning unit under their American Home Shield home warranty. The agent confirmed the issue as a possible low Freon and freezing lines, scheduled a preferred appointment for Sunday, and offered to contact American Home Shield for the dispatch number. The user was satisfied with the service and provided positive feedback.

Jason from Shirley Air Jack Cleaning returned a phone call and was greeted by Sophie from Budget. Sophie acknowledged the callback and transferred Jason to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer.
The user called to report receiving a call from Budget's number. The agent acknowledged the information and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Matthew Moynihan, called to schedule a service appointment for his AC which is freezing up. The agent confirmed his details and provided available time slots, but the user requested a time after 4 PM on Monday, which was noted. The call ended with the user still clarifying his preferred time.

The user called to follow up on a heating and cooling service request to have a technician return and add refrigerant and a leak prevention part called "cats" to their central air conditioner. The home warranty dispatch number was not available, and the user requested the earliest possible service. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that someone would contact them shortly, and the user declined information about maintenance programs.

Pat called to schedule a routine maintenance checkup for their air conditioning system. The agent provided available time slots, and Pat booked an appointment for April 15th at 2 PM. The call ended positively with Pat giving a high satisfaction rating and positive feedback about the agent's assistance.

The user, Tiffany Lowery, called to verify her appointment schedule but the agent could not find her appointment in the system. Tiffany chose to leave a message requesting to schedule an appointment for next week, either Tuesday or Wednesday. The agent assured her that the message would be forwarded to the team for follow-up.

The agent, Sophie, contacted Deborah who mentioned she had received a message but had not checked it yet. Deborah decided to check the message and call back later, and the agent offered to connect her to a live agent immediately before transferring the call successfully.
The user, Sunita from Assured Home Warranty, called and requested to speak to the office manager. The agent attempted to connect her to a live agent and successfully transferred the call.
The user requested to speak with a person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Jett called to request that a report from a technician be filed with their home warranty regarding their heating and cooling system, which needs to be replaced. The agent confirmed the details and assured Jett that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly to assist. Jett declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user requested to be connected to CJ, and the agent successfully transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.
The user Maddie returned a call from Nicole and was connected to a live agent for further assistance. The call was transferred successfully to another agent for handling.

The user provided their service address ZIP code and mentioned the company's location in Wentzville, noting the distance might cause delays. The agent then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Liza from American Home Shield, called to request the immediate cancellation of a work order for air conditioning service. The agent confirmed the details and assured the user that the cancellation request would be passed to the team for processing. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the interaction.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance. No additional information was provided by the user.

The user, Jaclyn Jones, called to sign up for a whole home plan and schedule routine HVAC cleaning and maintenance, including an estimate for a new thermostat and outdoor unit. An appointment was successfully booked for April 15th at 10 AM. Additionally, the user requested a plumbing appointment for drain cleaning, which was noted and will be confirmed by a callback from the team.

Lori Noker called to review an estimate for her furnace HVAC unit and requested a service appointment anytime today. The agent provided available slots for April 14th and 15th, but Lori asked to speak to someone else, leading to a call transfer. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

The user, Jim Stigemeyer, called to schedule a service appointment for a heat pump showing an error code at his apartment building. The agent confirmed the details and booked an appointment for April 14, 2026, at 2 PM. The user confirmed the booking and the call ended successfully.

The user, Daniel from Nexar, called to introduce his company's dashcam and GPS tracking services and inquired if the agent's company currently uses dashcams. The agent requested a service ZIP code to assist further, which the user provided, but the call ended abruptly after the user repeated questions without receiving a response.

The user called to address heating and cooling issues in two rooms of a recent addition to their home. The agent gathered necessary details, confirmed the user's information, and successfully booked a service appointment for April 13th between 2 PM and 4 PM. The user declined to provide feedback and ended the call.

Megan Rowe inquired about the average cost of a split system unit installation. The agent provided pricing information and offered to have a team member follow up, but Megan decided to have someone else handle the installation and did not provide the service address. The call ended with Megan confirming the company does installations and expressing thanks.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Jose called to inquire about the delay of a scheduled appointment that was supposed to occur between 9 and 11 AM but had not started by after 12 PM. The agent confirmed the contact number and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer.
The user called to reconnect with a live agent after a previous call was cut off. The agent acknowledged the issue and successfully transferred the user to the appropriate live agent.
